title: Level 19.2/Offset1
wikitext:
🗕 🗗 [[Backrooms Wiki|🗙]]
---- [[Level 253|←]] [[Level 255|→]] [[Level 19.2|↻]] [[Backrooms Wiki|⌂]]
🔓︎ www.MEG.com/leveldatabase?clusterone/levels_0_to_99/file:Level_19.2?action=read
{{Audio|file=Sourire d'Avril - Maurice Depret.mp3}} {{Background|file=Level 254-3.png}} {{DISPLAYTITLE:'''Level 19.2: "Paradisal Halls"'''}} {{Class Custom Image|class=Habitable|hexagon=⚘|header=Class: |safety=Entirely Safe|security=Entirely Stable|entity=Sustained Communities|bg-image=Level254-2.png|bg-size=1300px|text-color=lime}} == '''Description''' == {{Blurimage|file=Level254-2.png|caption=A typical hallway in Level 19.2, with another wanderer in frame.|hovertext=Wander upon paradisal halls.}} {{Blurimage|file=Level 254-3.png|caption=A recently discovered hallway in Level 19.2.|hovertext=Let yourself relax.}} Level 19.2 is a sublevel of [[Level 19]]. 「Level 19.2 is a sprawling complex of endless, cleanly hotel halls with ornate lighting. Wanderers always enter through a large, plush hotel room; however the specific details vary. Unlike other levels, lighting throughout the level stays constant, never flickering. Unlike most levels, lights are never mounted on the ceiling. Doors vary in material, ranging from wood to steel. Exit signs can be found occasionally throughout the level, usually turned on and granting the wanderer a deep sense of peace. The environment is uniquely stable throughout the level. While other levels may be non-Euclidean and have unstable architecture, Level 19.2 is uniquely linear and is quite stable. 」 「 Conditions within the level are notably temperate. The temperature fluctuates wildly from around 21°C70°F to 32°C89.6°F. This, alongside the stable architecture, makes this level highly suitable for human life. To add on to these benefits, supplies can be consistently and frequently, such as [[Object 1|Almond Water]] and [[Object 400|Work Juice]]. The wallpaper appears to positively affect the wanderer, as being near the walls fills the wanderer with a sense of security. Locked steel doors appear across the level, offering an exit; however these exits usually lead to peaceful but lower-quality levels like [[Level 4]]. It is believed these doors are direct holes in [[The Void|the void]] to transition so seamlessly. 「 The hotel rooms other than the one wanderers spawn in are always fully furnished, and contain significantly more supplies. Any attempt to break or damage a painting results in the painting instantly regenerating and a small plastic bag of non-anomalous snacks appearing on the floor, as if it were an offering. Heaters appear rarely, but become more often in the winter months, providing sufficient heat to stay warm and cozy. Hotel rooms beyond the initial spawn point are far more beneficial, frequently containing coolers with beverages, and pantries stocked with snacks and other foodstuffs. Survival within this environment is uniquely easy, as minimal threats exist within the level. 」 == Entities == 「 Other than humans and flora, no life exists here. 」 == Phenomena == {{Phenomenon Class|frequency=~4.9|effectiveness=5|danger=0}} The Level 19.2 Effect, also known as Forgetfulness, is a phenomenon where people under its influence wholeheartedly believe that the level is vastly different to its true form. People affected by the Level 19.2 Effect cannot be swayed and will be steadfast in their beliefs, regardless of its detriment to personal bonds or leadership position. While the phenomenon is otherwise benign, sadly roughly 98% of all Backrooms denizens are affected, including high-ranking officials in multiple groups. Surprisingly, beliefs of the people affected are usually concrete, and affected persons believe: * That Level 19.2 is dangerous and uninhabitable, which it is not; * That Level 19.2 has a derelict and dilapidated appearance, in contrast to its cleanly and well-ordered state; * That Level 19.2 has one nomadic community, which it does not; * And that Level 19.2 is a horrible place to live, which it is certainly not. Formerly believed to be misremembering the current state of [[Level 350|Level 350]], this has been disproven, as newcomers who had never learned of Level 19.2's existence nor [[Level 350|350]]'s still adamantly believed these erroneous facts. These individuals should be disregarded on matters pertaining to the level. == Colonies/Outposts == === M.E.G. Base Xenia=== *Official M.E.G. base with ~40 members *Accepting of new members *Dedicated to researching the level and gathering supplies *Open to trading === The Minicolony=== *First and only outpost of [[Level 399#Communities, Entities, Entrances, and Exits|the Megacolony]]. **Not actually official, but they get most supplies from Level 399. *Accepting of new members, currently ~3000 people *Dedicated to gathering supplies and creating a haven *Open to trading === M.E.G. Outpost Filoxenia=== *Research division of Base Xenia *Occasionally accepts new members, ~27 members *Dedicated to researching the level *Open to trading === Colony Group=== *Not a single colony, grouping of various colonies with a shared leader *Accepting new members depends on the colony, ~1,930 members *Dedicated to creating a haven *Openness to trading depends on the colony == Entrances and Exits == === Entrances === Entering abnormally plush and well-kempt hotel rooms in any hotel-based level (ex. [[Level 5]], [[Level 350]]) and falling asleep will lead one to wake up here. Entering a vent in '''"The Residual Space"''' of [[Level 960]] also leads one here. Certain factors may cause levels with a primarily hallway-based layout may lead here. Notably, in [[Level 634]], a door affixed with 'HOTEL' scrawled on in sharpie may appear; opening it will knock one out and cause them to wake up in the level. === Exits === Doors affixed with a 19.2 will lead to [[Level 525|525252525252525252525252]]. Doors affixed with [[Level 332|a brass number '6']] will lead to the aforementioned level. Doors affixed with the phrase 'Front Desk' may lead to one of the check-in counters in [[Level 187|Level 187]]. Doors affixed with the phrase 'Hotel Pool' may lead to [[Level 330|Level 330]]. {{Collapse|initial=NEW NOTIFICATION!|final=No new notifications.|body= This file has been updated. View new file? [[Level 19.2|Y]] N}} {{Collapse|initial=Open Author Information?|final=Close Author Information?|body= This level was created by [[User:Thebackroomsloverwholoveswiki||TheBackroomsLoverWhoLovesWiki]] Later massively revamped by [[User:JakitoFan|Yours truly]]. }} === References === [[Category:Levels]] [[Category:Cluster I]] [[Category:Dangerous]] [[zh:Level 19.2/Offset1]]
